metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email includes all required key facts accurately and naturally.

rubric.description: The email ignores the required facts or contradicts them.
Only a tiny fragment of one required fact appears.
Some facts are present, but multiple important facts are missing or distorted.
Most facts are present, but one important fact is missing, weakened, or ambiguous.
Almost all required facts are present, with only a minor omission or awkward compression.
Every required fact is included accurately and naturally, with no unsupported factual claims.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email matches the requested tone in context.

rubric.description: The tone is absent, opposite of the requested tone, or unusable for the context.
The tone is very far from the requested tone.
The tone only loosely resembles the requested tone and is noticeably mismatched.
The tone is partly aligned but generic, uneven, or inconsistent.
The tone is mostly aligned with only small mismatches.
The tone closely and consistently matches the requested tone throughout.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the output works as a professional email.

rubric.description: The output is not usable as an email.
It barely resembles a professional email or collapses greeting, body, closing, and signature into a flat single paragraph.
Some email elements are present, but structure is incomplete, hard to scan, or mostly unformatted.
Basic email content is present, but paragraphing, greeting, body, or sign-off formatting is noticeably weak.
The email is clear and professional with only minor structure or formatting rough edges.
The subject and body are polished, clear, concise, and formatted with distinct greeting, body paragraphing, and sign-off.

metrics.reason: The email includes all required facts accurately: it asks Sarah Whalen for exact waiting instructions, names the attendees Kenneth Lay, Marty Sunde, and Bob Williams, specifies the May 17 meeting at 11:00 A.M., and clearly states the sender needs to know where they should wait or go when they arrive. No required fact is missing or distorted.

The email closely and consistently matches the requested tone: it is concise, polite, and professional throughout. It uses a direct request, courteous wording, and an appropriate closing without unnecessary detail or informal language.

The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, a concise body in a separate paragraph, and an appropriate sign-off with signature. The structure is polished and professional, with no meaningful formatting weaknesses.

All required facts are present and accurately stated: the meeting is for next week, the three proposed time slots match the scenario, recipients are asked to indicate their availability for those options, and the schedule is to be finalized soon. No required fact is missing or contradicted.

The email closely and consistently matches the requested professional, concise, and collaborative tone. It is polite and direct, uses clear scheduling language, invites input from the team, and keeps the message brief without unnecessary detail.

The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, properly separated body content with a short introductory paragraph and a bulleted list, a closing sign-off, and a signature with name and title. It is well formatted and professional with no meaningful structural weaknesses.

The email includes all required facts accurately: it identifies invoice 48217, states it was due on April 30, 2026, gives the outstanding balance as $3,450, and explicitly requests an update on the expected payment date. It is also addressed appropriately to the Accounts Payable Team and signed by Nina Brooks, Procurement Coordinator at Harborview Labs.

The email is polite and professional throughout, with courteous language like “Could you please” and “I would appreciate,” while still being firm enough to clearly ask for an update on the overdue invoice. It matches the requested tone well, though the firmness is mild rather than strongly assertive, so it is not a perfect fit.

The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, a polite closing, and a signature with name and affiliation. The formatting is polished and professional with no meaningful structural weaknesses.

All required facts are present and accurate: the original interview time was Tuesday at 2:00 PM, there is a conflict on the interviewer’s side, the alternatives offered are Wednesday at 9:00 AM or Thursday at 3:00 PM, the interview is for the Software Engineer position at BrightPath Software Solutions, it is expected to last about 90 minutes, and the candidate is asked to confirm which time works.

The email is consistently professional, courteous, apologetic, and concise: it opens directly with a polite apology, states the rescheduling request clearly, offers alternative times, and closes with a brief expression of thanks and understanding.

The email is clearly structured with a subject, greeting, body, and sign-off/signature, and it is easy to scan. However, the body is only a single paragraph and the formatting is straightforward rather than polished, so it falls short of a perfect 5.

The email includes the early-bird deadline of September 10, 2025, the conference dates of October 22 to 24, 2025, both registration fees ($300 early-bird and $400 regular), and a clear encouragement to register soon to secure the lower rate. The only key fact not explicitly reflected is the current date of September 5, 2025, which is a minor omission for this reminder.

The email is consistently informative, upbeat, and professional: it uses a friendly reminder, clearly states the deadline and pricing, and ends with a polite professional closing. The tone matches the requested style throughout, with only minor generic phrasing that keeps it from being a perfect 5.

The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, separate body paragraphs, a professional closing, and a signature block. The formatting is polished and easy to scan, with no meaningful structural weaknesses.

The email includes all three required facts accurately: it states the design review is expected to finish by Friday, 19 June, 2026, explains the delay was caused by missing stakeholder feedback, and says no change is needed to the final launch date yet. There are no factual omissions or contradictions.

The email is transparent about the delay and its cause, and the wording stays calm and reassuring throughout. Phrases like "no change is needed to the final launch date" and "we will continue to monitor progress" reinforce a steady, reassuring tone. It is slightly generic, so it does not fully rise to a perfect 5.

The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, separate body paragraphs, and a proper closing with signature placeholder. The formatting is polished and easy to scan, with no meaningful structural weaknesses.

The email includes all required facts: it references the Q3 roadmap priorities meeting, states that the attached notes summarize the key decisions and action items, and asks for corrections by end of day Thursday. It also preserves the intended sender, recipient, and professional framing without adding unsupported claims.

The email is clear, organized, and professional throughout: it uses a polite greeting, concise meeting-summary language, a direct request for corrections by Thursday, and a formal closing with the sender’s name and title.

The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, a well-formed body in separate paragraphs, and a professional closing/signature. It clearly mentions the Q3 roadmap meeting notes, states that the attached notes summarize key decisions and action items, and asks for corrections by end of day Thursday. Formatting is polished and matches the professional_structure rubric for a top score.
All required facts are present accurately: the cleanup is on Saturday from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM, volunteers are asked to bring gloves and closed-toe shoes, and lunch and water will be provided.

The email consistently uses a friendly, encouraging, and appreciative tone throughout, with welcoming language (“We’re excited to invite you”), supportive phrasing (“Your help will make a real difference”), and gratitude (“We truly appreciate your support”). It matches the requested tone closely and without meaningful mismatch.

The email has a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, well-separated body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off with signature and affiliation. The formatting is polished and professional with no meaningful structural weaknesses.

All three required facts are stated clearly and accurately: visitors must check in at the front desk, a government-issued photo ID is required for entry, and the policy starts on Monday. No required fact is missing or distorted.

The email consistently uses a formal, informative tone throughout: it opens with a polished notice ('Please be advised'), clearly states the policy update and effective date, and closes with a courteous professional thanks. There are no casual, emotional, or off-tone elements.

The email includes a clear subject line, a distinct greeting, a well-formatted body, and separate closing/signature lines. The structure is clean, professional, and easy to scan with no meaningful formatting weaknesses.

The email includes all required facts accurately: maintenance is scheduled for Sunday from 1:00 AM to 3:00 AM, the portal may be unavailable during that window, and service is expected to resume immediately after maintenance. The tone is brief, reassuring, and professional, with no unsupported claims.

The email is brief, professional, and reassuring throughout, with polite wording about the maintenance and expected recovery. It is consistent, though fairly standard and not especially distinctive, so it is mostly aligned rather than perfect.

The email includes a clear subject, a distinct greeting, two separate body paragraphs, and a proper sign-off with signature. It is well formatted, easy to scan, and matches the professional structure rubric closely.
